reviews.review_description: Love the game although I find the gun are a little off at times.  I’m a 62 year old grandmother and find it’s great exercise.  A little blurb on explaining the scoring and premise would be helpful. Thank you!

I recently purchased Unpatterned Cut from Quest Store and was pleasantly surprised by how immersive and rewarding the experience turned out to be. The game stands out with its cleverly increasing difficulty – each level pushes you to develop new strategies and sharpen your reflexes. It's not just about reacting quickly, but also about thinking differently as you progress, which kept me constantly engaged.

What truly adds to the atmosphere is the music. The soundtrack perfectly complements the gameplay, pulling you deeper into the world of Unpatterned Cut. And the story—mysterious and gradually unfolding—makes you want to keep going just to discover what’s next.

Overall, it’s a well-crafted game that challenges both your mind and reflexes while wrapping everything in a compelling aesthetic. Highly recommended for anyone looking for something unique and thought-provoking in VR.
Mix of beat saber and pistol whip? Yes please

After about an hour of play (just reached Chapter 3)  I generally liked the game. There's a satisfying learning curve: once you adapt to the slash pace and pistol aiming, everything clicks. New mechanics arrive right on time never lets you get bored. Also, good optimization.
Mechanics and adaptation to it kinda different. You have to stay in the game always. Also there cinematic story scenes which kind of different trial in rythim genre.
Cool indie stuff. Guns hit nice, worth what you pay
